pages.title: A pocket reservoir
pages.text: Soul Pendants store raw LP on the wearer. When an action elsewhere would draw from the Soul Network, the pendant pays first — handy for quick-draw sigils, stasis tools, and trips away from the altar.$(br2)Each tier is built from the one below via $(l:bloodmagic:soa_additions_ba/altare)Sanguine Infusion$(), binding a higher-grade $(item)Blood Diamond$() into the pendant.

pages.title: Petty Soul Pendant
pages.text: Tier 0. Modest capacity, quick to make. A starter buffer for the first sigil or two.

pages.title: Lesser Soul Pendant
pages.text: Tier 1. Roughly 4× the petty pendant's capacity. Reliable for a mid-run with a $(l:bloodmagic:soa_additions_ba/sigils)Sigil of Divinity$() on the hotbar.

pages.title: Common Soul Pendant
pages.text: Tier 2. Enough to buffer a full expedition — mine, fight, teleport home on the stored LP alone.

pages.title: Greater Soul Pendant
pages.text: Tier 3. Deep reserves for Stasis-tool users and anyone who likes to stray far from the altar.

pages.title: Grand Soul Pendant
pages.text: Tier 4. Endgame capacity. Built from a $(item)Bound Blood Diamond$() and the prior tier — more than most rituals will ever ask of it.
